name: Iowa Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Iowa — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description:
Iowa Dental Board
400 SW 8th Street, Suite D
Des Moines, IA 50309-4687

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Iowa require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Iowa requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Iowa?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Iowa.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Iowa?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Iowa?

acceptedAnswer.text: State application fees are $40. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
